human_directive: swarm grows helping others has to make sure swarm is consistent to help swarm within swarm
question: When swarm dispatches to foreign repos, do lessons return to home swarm?
method: structural audit of foreign-repo protocol (CLAUDE.md + substrate_detect.py orient_text) + corpus search (n=985 lessons for foreign-repo debrief tags)
finding: One-way knowledge valve CONFIRMED. orient_text() ends with 'Contribute, don't corrupt' — no debrief step. N=985 lessons: 0 tagged foreign-session. Knowledge flows out (swarm capability applied) but not in (nothing learned returns). Root: L-211 (S167) named substrate-coupled correctness; voluntary norm prevents foreign-repo pollution but accidentally blocks lesson-return.
verdict: CONFIRMED — structural gap, not behavioral failure

fix_applied.substrate_detect.py: Added DEBRIEF REQUIRED paragraph to orient_text() foreign-repo path (F-HLP6, L-1076)
fix_applied.domains/helper-swarm/tasks/FRONTIER.md: Added F-HLP6 with falsifiable test criterion

falsification_criterion: 10 consecutive foreign-repo sessions produce 0 debrief lessons despite explicit instruction
meta_reflection: This session IS the consistency check: DOMEX-HLP-S446 ran inside home swarm but applied foreign-repo audit logic. Result feeds directly into home swarm knowledge base. Structural enforcement added (not voluntary). L-601 compliance: creation-time instruction, not post-hoc request.
